label: Maya · campus cash flow reset
persona: 20-year-old university student balancing a part-time café job, rent, and tuition stress.
blurb: Great demo for showing how FinPath turns irregular student income into a practical emergency-fund plan.

profile.name: Maya
profile.concern: I want to stop feeling behind every month without giving up every social activity.

label: Jordan · first-job momentum
persona: 23-year-old recent graduate with a first full-time role, student debt, and a goal to move out confidently.
blurb: Shows how FinPath supports early-career users who have more income but need better allocation and goal pacing.

profile.name: Jordan
profile.concern: I make enough to save, but my money disappears before I feel real progress toward moving out.

label: Aisha · internship-to-full-time runway
persona: 22-year-old intern supporting family expenses while trying to build a certification fund and a cash cushion.
blurb: Highlights FinPath’s social-impact angle: practical planning for users juggling ambition, responsibility, and limited margin.

profile.name: Aisha
profile.concern: I want to grow my career options, help at home, and still feel safe if an unexpected bill shows up.
